reforms.description: The Slovak government aims to enhance the innovative capacity of the economy by promoting business innovation in domestically-owned firms and start-ups through improved tax treatment of capital gains and relaxing restrictions on pension funds investing in venture capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving tax incentives and access to venture capital, the reform is expected to stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to improve business innovation through tax reforms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in innovation policy, aiming to integrate global knowledge into the domestic economy, which is crucial for long-term growth.
reforms.source_quote: Re-orient innovation policy towards facilitating the dissemination of global knowledge in a wide range of manufacturing and service sectors.

reforms.description: The Slovak government has proposed a comprehensive strategy known as the 'Lisbon Strategy for Slovakia' aimed at enhancing competitiveness and innovation capacity through education and the development of a knowledge-based economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing on education and human capital, the strategy aims to improve productivity and innovation, which are essential for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined the necessary steps to achieve the goals of the strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant shift towards a knowledge-based economy, which is crucial for long-term economic development and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s competitiveness strategy also stresses the importance of strengthening the competitiveness and innovation-capacity of the economy through emphasising education and the building of a knowledge-based economy.

reforms.description: The Slovak government proposed to provide additional financial support for leading scientists and research institutions, as well as for PhD students and graduates, to enhance the country's research capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for research is expected to enhance the quality and quantity of scientific output, contributing to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has proposed a number of innovation policy initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards improving research funding in Slovakia, which is crucial for enhancing innovation outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: The government has proposed a number of innovation policy initiatives, including... additional financial support for leading Slovak scientists and research institutions as well as for PhD students and PhD graduates.

reforms.description: The Slovak government plans to introduce a system for public co-financing of private sector research, development, and innovations to stimulate collaboration between public and private entities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Co-financing is expected to leverage private investment in R&D, enhancing innovation and economic growth through increased collaboration.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has proposed a number of innovation policy initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the interaction between public and private sectors, which is essential for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has proposed a number of innovation policy initiatives, including... a system for public co-financing of private sector research, development and innovations.

reforms.description: The Slovak government aims to create a public instrument to improve access to venture capital for innovative firms in their early stages, addressing a critical funding gap.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to venture capital is expected to facilitate the growth of innovative startups, thereby contributing to economic dynamism and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has proposed a number of innovation policy initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is important as it targets a key barrier to startup growth, which is access to early-stage funding.
reforms.source_quote: The government has proposed a number of innovation policy initiatives, including... a public instrument to improve access to venture capital for innovative firms in the early stages of their operation.

reforms.description: The Slovak government proposed abolishing or applying capital gains tax only to windfall gains to encourage investment from retained earnings, which would enhance innovation activity among small domestic firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing tax burdens, the reform aims to stimulate investment and innovation in small firms, thereby enhancing overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal to abolish or modify the capital gains tax was discussed as part of the government's strategy to improve conditions for innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in tax policy aimed at fostering innovation, particularly among small firms, which are crucial for economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: First, the capital gains tax should be abolished or applied only to windfall capital gains, so as not to discourage investment from retained earnings.

reforms.description: The Slovak government aims to enhance the business environment by reducing administrative burdens and improving regulations, which is expected to foster innovation and growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining regulations, the reform is expected to lower barriers for businesses, encouraging innovation and new enterprise formation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has made significant progress in recent years to reduce unnecessary administrative burdens on businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses systemic issues in the regulatory environment that can hinder innovation and business growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Slovak government has made very significant progress in recent years to reduce unnecessary administrative burdens on businesses and to enhance competition in product markets.

reforms.description: The Slovak government plans to relax restrictions on pension funds and insurance companies regarding investments in venture capital and private equity funds to stimulate growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By allowing more investment in venture capital, the reform is expected to enhance funding availability for startups, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has proposed to relax investment restrictions to stimulate venture capital.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it opens new avenues for funding innovative firms, although it is not as comprehensive as a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Relax present restrictions on pension funds and insurance companies from investing in venture capital and private equity funds.

reforms.description: The Slovak government has implemented reforms to improve conditions for innovation and growth, focusing on fostering a competitive business environment and addressing deficiencies in the legal system and education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the business environment and addressing educational shortcomings, these reforms are expected to stimulate innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has actively pursued reforms to enhance innovation conditions.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms represent a significant shift in policy aimed at improving the overall innovation landscape in Slovakia, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation policies should focus on continuing to foster an efficient business environment for dynamic enterprise creation and competition.

reforms.description: The Slovak authorities have initiated a new 'Lisbon Strategy for Slovakia' aimed at improving human capital and education, facilitating innovative firms' access to equity finance, and developing information technology infrastructures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing human capital and access to finance is expected to stimulate innovation and economic growth by improving the capabilities of the workforce and supporting the establishment of new firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have stated their willingness to address these issues with the new strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant shift in policy focus towards enhancing human capital and innovation, which are critical for long-term economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have stated their willingness to address these issues, in particular with a new 'Lisbon Strategy for Slovakia' which aims to improve human capital and education, facilitate innovative firms’ access to equity finance, develop information technology infrastructures, and improve the general business environment.

reforms.description: A new housing policy was introduced to improve inter-regional labour mobility by simplifying the transaction tax on house sales and providing a lump benefit for those relocating for work.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing labour mobility can lead to a more efficient allocation of resources and boost overall productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The transaction tax was simplified and eliminated as of January 2005.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses a significant barrier to labour mobility, which is crucial for adapting to structural changes in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Some steps have already been taken to improve labour mobility. Importantly, the transaction tax on house sales was simplified to a flat rate of 3% from January 2004 and eliminated completely as of January 2005.

reforms.description: The Slovak government aims to enhance the innovative capacity of firms by promoting policies that raise the economy's ability to absorb existing knowledge, which is crucial for productivity and employment growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the capacity of firms to innovate, the policy is expected to boost productivity and create more employment opportunities.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has identified innovation policy as a key long-term priority.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy focus towards enhancing innovation, which is critical for the country's economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s Lisbon agenda – which identifies innovation policy as one of the government’s key long-term priorities – defines innovation as the effective combination of science, research and innovations.

reforms.description: The Slovak government plans to facilitate the entry of new high-quality tertiary institutions to improve education outcomes and promote competition in the higher education sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing competition and quality in higher education, the reform is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, which is vital for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: It was hoped that the Tertiary Funding Bill would increase student demands for quality improvements.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major initiative aimed at transforming the higher education landscape, which is crucial for developing human capital and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Another way is to raise students’ expectations. It was hoped that the Tertiary Funding Bill, by permitting the introduction of partial tuition fees, would increase student demands for quality improvements.

reforms.description: Slovakia has been trying to introduce results-oriented budgeting since 2002 to subject public activities to explicit objectives, cost transparency, and performance tests, aiming to use the budget as a strategic resource allocation instrument.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing budget transparency and accountability, this reform aims to improve the efficiency of public spending, which can lead to better resource allocation and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has decided to give a new impulse to results-oriented budgeting.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards a more strategic approach in public finance management, which can enhance the effectiveness of public services and resource allocation.
reforms.source_quote: The introduction of results-oriented budgeting in which the budget process is centred around well-defined and measurable goals will play a crucial role.

reforms.description: Slovakia is introducing market mechanisms in publicly-funded services, including user fees and competitive provision, to enhance efficiency and accountability in sectors like education and health.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By introducing market mechanisms, the initiative aims to improve service delivery and reduce excess demand, which can lead to better resource allocation and efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: Slovakia has begun to introduce market mechanisms to make these services more effective and more cost-efficient.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards market-oriented approaches in public service delivery, which could enhance efficiency and accountability.
reforms.source_quote: Slovakia has begun to introduce market mechanisms to make these services more effective and more cost-efficient.

reforms.description: The introduction of results-oriented budgeting in primary and secondary education aims to set objectives based on actual academic outcomes, enhancing accountability and performance.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing on outcomes, this reform is expected to improve educational quality and student performance, which can contribute to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The introduction of results-oriented budgeting at the municipal and regional levels would permit objectives to be set regarding the actual outcomes of pupils.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance accountability in education, which is crucial for improving overall educational outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: The introduction of results-oriented budgeting at the municipal and regional levels would permit objectives to be set regarding the actual outcomes of pupils.

reforms.description: The government is exploring the introduction of student fees in tertiary education to improve capacity and encourage competition among educational institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Introducing student fees could enhance funding for universities and stimulate competition, leading to improved educational quality and innovation in teaching methods.
reforms.status_evidence: It is disappointing that the Government’s efforts to introduce student fees failed twice in Parliament, in 2004 and 2005.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses funding challenges in higher education and aims to enhance competition and quality in the sector.
reforms.source_quote: It is disappointing that the Government’s efforts to introduce student fees (even when backed by the sweetener of generous student grants) failed twice in Parliament.
